retry_reason: Unconditional put operations have no failure path; retry is not applicable.
contract_summary: This function drains a ptr_ring during cleanup, releasing all references to its entries. Each entry is either an skb (freed via kfree_skb) or an xdp_frame (freed via xdp_return_frame). The function is idempotent and will consume all entries, warning if any were present (indicating a prior bug). After calling this function, the ring will be empty but not freed; callers should ensure the ring is no longer in use before calling this function. No reference is returned to the caller; the function solely performs release operations.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to release references to skbs and xdp frames stored in the ring, draining it. The only logic is freeing objects; no other business logic.

extract.reason: For the xdp_frame path in __cpu_map_ring_cleanup, xdp_return_frame calls __xdp_return which in turn calls put_page or page_pool_put_full_netmem. To trace the complete member_access_path and type_chain from the function's interface to the refcount field, we need to see how these callees manipulate the refcount (e.g., which field on which struct). Currently, only kfree_skb's callee info is provided, so we lack the refcount details for the xdp_frame path.
